title: Sanji Faces the Vinsmokes
description: On pages 15–18, this part focuses on Unknown person #13, Unknown person #14, Big Mom Pirates, Big Mom, Unknown person #15, Unknown person #16 as The Straw Hats enter Big Mom’s strange territory while Sanji faces the Vinsmoke family and the arranged marriage.
scene: Sanji is forced back into the Vinsmoke family’s control, revealing the cruelty behind his royal bloodline.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: ...so that's why I've been interrogat- ing the big guy over here!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You must've been watching the whole time... where are chopper and carrot?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: care to explain why neither that woman bro!lee nor her weird tree henchmen didn't harm a single hair on your head?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The only logical explanation is that you're their ACCOMP- LICE!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #13
dialogue.text: Me... Their ally...?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Well... I guess I wouldn't exactly call them my enemies.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I'll gladly tell you all I know... but first, walk over in that direction to your left, and...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Grab me some of that sweet, sweet apple juice...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #13
dialogue.text: We don't have time for that!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But... If you actually tell us something useful,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I'll be happy to get you all the juice your heart desires!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Of course! Besides, you've been talking about it so much that I kinda wanna try drinking some too!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #13
dialogue.text: You mean that?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: By the way, you... you seem pretty strong...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #13
dialogue.text: But you... not so much...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #13
dialogue.text: WHATA...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I'll have you know I am!! Very much so! Why do you even ask?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I see, I see... I guess you can't judge a book by its cover!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #14
dialogue.text: that Reindeer guy was obscenely strong too...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But those trees still pounced on him without the slightest bit of hesitation!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Those guys really never stop until they've accomplished their mission.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #14
dialogue.text: Now that you mention it...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: What made those guys decide to stop pursuing me after a while?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: In my case, after I beat the living crap out of 'em. They stopped harassing me!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: How is it that they're able to move in the first place?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #14
dialogue.text: BA- DUM

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #14
dialogue.text: Hmmm, well...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Here in "Totto Land", every 6 months, each citizen is required to pay a toll...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: A month's worth of their "soul" given to the nation, in exchange for safety and protection.

dialogue.speaker: Big Mom Pirates
dialogue.text: ...soul?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Ah, well basically... they have to give up part of their lifespan. Everyone who lives here loses 2 months of their lives in a single year,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #14
dialogue.text: and a year's worth in 6.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #14
dialogue.text: How is that even possible?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Ah, I mean big mom... is an ability user who ate the "soul soul fruit".

dialogue.speaker: Big Mom
dialogue.text: Well, you see, Linlin...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: She's able to freely interact with people's "sous".

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: With that power, she takes some time out of the citizens' lifespans,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: and sprinkles those "human souls" all throughout the island.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Once this is done, the souls settle into random objects or things and they end up getting "humanized"! Just to be clear, the souls cannot enter a corpse or the body of "somebody else".

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: So those guys standing over there are just some of the things that those souls I mentioned earlier decided to settle inside.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #15
dialogue.text: They're called the "Homes".

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: As for the collection and assign- went of these pieces of soul...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: That's all handled by the "incarnations" made out of Linlin... I mean, big mom's own soul.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: That is the secret behind the reality here.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: ...that chocolate town we visited earlier also had lots of living things that had no business being alive...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #15
dialogue.text: Now it's all starting to make sense!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #15
dialogue.text: What an outrageous ability!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Is that why we came across a talking crocodile?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #15
dialogue.text: And that humankind rabbit too...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Yep, that is correct. Nothing stops the souls from settling into animals.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Wait, so is that what's going on with these guys too?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #16
dialogue.text: That's an entirely different story.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Those are just some animals that were trans- formed...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #16
dialogue.text: by br[lee's ability.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You could have said so earlier!! I mean, you were right here the whole time!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Yeah but... I really didn't want to get involved!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: If you actually don't want to be involved, why did you bury yourself here?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #16
dialogue.text: Someone knows I

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #16
dialogue.text: Why?! Who the hell are you, anyway?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Just spit it out and I'll get you some juice!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #16
dialogue.text: Someone remember

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Well, long ago ...way, way, way in the past...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I... was once the husband of Charlotte Linlin... otherwise known as the pirate "Big Mom".

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #16
dialogue.text: What?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But once I gave her two daughters together... she threw me to the curb...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: As for the reason why nobody attacks me... well, the truth is, nobody really gives a damn about me!

summary: This section advances the Whole Cake Island storyline with 66 dialogue beats. Whole Cake Island becomes a hostile fairy-tale battlefield where Sanji’s rescue and Big Mom’s power are both unavoidable.
